case_name: Street v. Upper Chesapeake Med. Ctr.

summary.brief: Street v. Upper Chesapeake Med. Ctr.. Medical malpractice case involving obstetrics gynaecology.

evidence_index.text: The appellant was seen in emergency room with complaints of her right foot being cool and pale, with symptoms increasing when leg elevated.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Emergency medicine doctor found pedal pulses but ankle brachial index score for the right foot was abnormal.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: She advised the appellant to see a vascular surgeon in three to five days, giving her a name and contact information.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Two days later the appellant returned to the emergency room with increased symptoms and was admitted to the hospital.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: A vascular surgeon was called for a consult, which was not labeled 'stat,' but did not see the appellant that day or the next.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: The appellant was suffering from right lower extremity ischemia due to diminished arterial blood flow.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: She underwent a below-the-knee amputation of the right leg.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: The trial court precluded the appellant’s board-certified vascular surgery expert from testifying that the emergency medicine physician breached the standard of care.
evidence_index.speaker: Judge's conclusion

evidence_index.text: The trial court’s judgments affirmed in part and vacated in part.
evidence_index.speaker: Judge's conclusion

evidence_index.text: Dr. Sumpio will opine based on his knowledge, training, and experience with respect to consults that he is routinely asked to perform in both the emergency room and hospital settings.
evidence_index.speaker: Expert witness

evidence_index.text: Dr. Sumpio will further opine to a reasonable degree of medical probability that Dr. Lu’s failure to consult with vascular surgery was a cause of Mrs. Street’s injuries and damages.
evidence_index.speaker: Expert witness

evidence_index.text: JUDGMENT OF THE CIRCUIT COURT FOR HARFORD COUNTY IN FAVOR OF UPPER CHESAPEAKE MEDICAL CENTER, INC., UPPER CHESAPEAKE EMERGENCY MEDICINE PHYSICIANS, LLC, AND LE NHA LU, M.D. AFFIRMED.
evidence_index.speaker: Judge's conclusion

evidence_index.text: JUDGMENT OF THE CIRCUIT COURT FOR HARFORD COUNTY IN FAVOR OF VASCULAR SURGERY ASSOCIATES, LLC, AND MARK GONZE, M.D. VACATED ONLY WITH RESPECT TO ALLEGATIONS OF MEDICAL MALPRACTICE ON JUNE 18, 2017, AND JUNE 19, 2017.
evidence_index.speaker: Judge's conclusion

initial_state.chief_complaint: Right foot being cool and pale
initial_state.history_of_present_illness: Patient reports right foot cool and pale, symptoms increase when leg elevated. Recent history of sciatica-type back pain and right foot numbness after enteroscopy.

focused_exam.lower_extremity: Right foot pale and cool, no palpable pulses, cyanotic toenails, normal sensation and motor strength, palpable pulses bilaterally when seated or lying down

initial_state.initial_working_problem: Diminished arterial blood flow to right leg
critical_findings: Abnormal ABI score of 0.6 in right dorsal pulse

requestables.name: Ultrasound
requestables.description: Ultrasound to check for deep vein thrombosis

reveal.result_summary: No evidence of deep vein thrombosis, ABI score of 0.6 in right dorsal pulse

requestables.name: Vascular Surgery Consultation
requestables.description: Consultation with vascular surgeon for right lower extremity ischemia

reveal.result_summary: Consultation not performed on June 18 or 19
reveal.clinical_significance: Delay in consultation contributed to worsening ischemia
reveal.decision_impact: Timely consultation could have altered management

timeline_phases.name: Initial Presentation
timeline_phases.description: Patient presents to ED with right foot symptoms

timeline_phases.name: Workup and Discharge
timeline_phases.description: Patient evaluated and discharged with follow-up instructions

key_events.event: Patient returns to ED with increased symptoms

decision_points.clinical_context: Patient in ED with right foot ischemia
decision_points.prompt: Should a vascular surgery consultation be ordered immediately?

options.clinical_reasoning: Immediate consultation needed due to abnormal ABI and ischemic symptoms

options.description: Advise follow-up with vascular surgery in 3-5 days
options.clinical_reasoning: Symptoms not deemed emergent by defendant

actual_action_defendant.reasoning_stated: Defendant did not see evidence of emergent vascular compromise

expected_action_court.description: Immediate vascular surgery consultation should have been ordered
expected_action_court.standard_of_care_basis: Expert testimony indicated need for immediate consultation

explanation.why_defendant_wrong: Delay in consultation led to worsening ischemia
explanation.what_should_have_happened: Immediate consultation could have prevented progression
explanation.legal_standard_applied: Standard of care as established by expert testimony

patient_outcome.description: Patient underwent below-the-knee amputation of the right leg
patient_outcome.long_term_effects: Loss of limb, potential for prosthetic use

key_findings: Failure to perform timely vascular consult
Causation established for progression to amputation

malpractice_determination.point_of_failure: Failure to order stat vascular consult
malpractice_determination.counterfactual: Timely consult could have prevented amputation

tests_performed.result: ABI score of 0.6 in right dorsal pulse

allegations.allegation: Failure to order immediate vascular consultation
Failure to provide timely vascular consultation
Failure to order stat vascular consult

court_findings.finding: No breach of standard of care by Dr. Lu
No breach of duty by Dr. Lu

court_findings.legal_significance: Failure to meet standard of care
